_description: Audit log of technical decisions and corrections. Append only.
author: [github-user-email]

_format.author: [github-user-email]
_format.type: decision | bugfix | config | security | naming | reviewer_correction

decisions.author: david.valdez@arroyoconsulting.net
decisions.title: NEXA auth security parameters are defined in NFR-SEC-001, not in an ADR
decisions.what: Argon2id (mem>=64MB, iter>=3, parallelism>=1), JWT access 15min + refresh rotation, 30-min inactivity timeout, account lockout after 5 failures/15-min progressive. Argon2id lib is Konscious.Security.Cryptography (bcrypt/PBKDF2 banned by SAD technical constraint).
decisions.why: Concrete values live in documentation/04-architecture/design-doc.md security table + NFR-SEC-001; the backlog's citation of 'SAD ADR-005' for Argon2id is WRONG (ADR-005 = Roslyn analyzers).
decisions.learned: When writing the US-001 BRD, cite Argon2id from the tech-doc technical constraint + NFR-SEC-001, never from ADR-005.

decisions.author: david.valdez@arroyoconsulting.net
decisions.title: US-001 authentication lives in the Core Monolith Auth Module, not a standalone service
decisions.what: Authoritative SAD (Alternative C — Hybrid Split) places auth as the Auth Module inside the Core Monolith at /src/Nexa.Auth/. The older design-doc's 'Auth Service' framing is superseded.
decisions.why: SAD-001 is the approved final architecture; design-doc.md predates the final split decision.
decisions.learned: Target /src/Nexa.Auth/ (Controllers/Services/Models) + /src/Nexa.Data.Core/ (core.* schema) for auth implementation. End-user login is NOT Entra ID (Entra is service-to-service only).

decisions.author: david.valdez@arroyoconsulting.net
decisions.title: NEXA JWT signing = RS256 (resolves HMAC-vs-RS256 doc conflict)
decisions.what: JWT signed with RS256 (asymmetric). Documented in ADR-2026-07-20-JWT-Signing-Algorithm-RS256.
decisions.why: design-doc security table + WBS 2.3.3 (RS256 key in Key Vault) win over SAD 'HMAC-signed' wording and WBS 3.1.1 '(HMAC RS256)' typo. Asymmetric lets APIM validate with public key without holding the signing secret.
decisions.learned: Signing key nexa-jwt-signing-key (RSA private) in Key Vault; APIM validates with public key.

decisions.author: david.valdez@arroyoconsulting.net
decisions.title: Auth transactional email goes DIRECT via IEmailSender, not Dispatch
decisions.what: Password-reset (and later new-user invitation) emails are sent by the Auth Module directly through IEmailSender/SendGrid, NOT enqueued through the compliance-gated Dispatch Module.
decisions.why: Dispatch/ServiceBus/Suppression are Sprint 2; auth email is Sprint 1 and is security-critical transactional mail exempt from marketing suppression/opt-out. FRS traceability maps FR-AA-012 to 'Auth Service / IEmailSender Adapter'. The 'only Dispatch calls providers' rule protects journey/mass sends only.
decisions.learned: Reviewer must ensure Auth never does bulk sends or bypasses compliance gate for non-transactional email. SendGrid SDK stays in the adapter; Auth depends on IEmailSender interface.

decisions.author: david.valdez@arroyoconsulting.net
decisions.title: OpenAPI contract for /auth/* is a US-001 deliverable to unblock US-025
decisions.what: Publish a code-generated OpenAPI 3 document for /auth/* (Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi native in .NET 10, or Swashbuckle). Also: password policy default (12/complexity/breached/history 5, PO-overridable); reset link TTL 60min single-use hashed; account lockout 5/15min added as AC-007.
decisions.why: OpenAPI is neither mandated nor prohibited anywhere in documentation/ (verified). Lets Developer 2 build US-025 login UI in parallel. OSS tooling complies with the licensed-runtime ban.
decisions.learned: US-001 is backend/API only; UX_Booster skipped (no UI surface, UI is US-025). Greenfield: no src/ or .sln existed; US-001 bootstraps the whole solution (R-002 effort risk).
decisions.confidence_note: OpenAPI location (repo/APIM/pipeline) still TBD with Tech Lead.
